title: When Thunder Counts to Seven
synopsis: When a summer storm approaches the pebble beach, young Malik notices that the thunder's counting game has gone mysteriously quiet. As waves crash and wind whips, he discovers that even the storm needs help sometimes, and that the most important victories happen when no one's keeping score.

pages.text:
Flash goes the lightning bright,
Six... five... four... three... two...
But seven never comes tonight,
Seven's lost, what should we do?

Malik counts along with storm,
On the beach of singing stones,
Where the pebbles dance and warm
To the thunder's rolling bones.

But the counting's gone all wrong,
Seven's missing from the song.

illustration.description: Malik, a boy with short twists, dark brown skin, and gentle expressive eyes, stands on a pebble beach with glowing stones around his feet. Dark storm clouds swirl overhead with lightning illuminating the scene. The enchanted forest art style shows mystical purple and green hues with golden sparkles dancing through the stormy air, Studio Ghibli inspired magical atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Malik counting during a storm on a magical pebble beach

pages.text:
Flash goes the lightning bright,
Six... five... four... three... two...
Then silence in the stormy night,
Where seven used to thunder through.

Mama counts but doesn't see
That the pattern's incomplete,
Daddy hums along with glee
To the storm's familiar beat.

But Malik knows what's missing here,
Seven's song should crystal clear.

illustration.description: Malik, a boy with short twists, dark brown skin, and gentle expressive eyes, looks thoughtful while his parents enjoy the storm in the background. The pebble beach glows with mystical creatures hiding between the stones. Rich greens and purples fill the stormy sky with golden sparkles, enchanted forest art style, detailed foliage swaying in the magical wind, Studio Ghibli inspired.
illustration.alt: Malik noticing something his parents miss during the storm

pages.text:
This morning on the pebble shore,
Seven thundered loud and proud,
Counting games like never before,
Bellowing strong above the crowd.

Each number had its special place,
Like players on a favorite team,
Seven always won the race
With thunder's most tremendous scream.

But something happened in between,
Now seven's nowhere to be seen.

illustration.description: A flashback scene showing Malik, a boy with short twists, dark brown skin, and gentle expressive eyes, earlier that morning on the same pebble beach under sunny skies. Magical numbers float visibly in the air around him, glowing with fairy lights. The enchanted forest art style shows the beach in bright golden morning light with mystical creatures playing among the detailed foliage, Studio Ghibli inspired magical atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Malik remembering when all the thunder numbers were present

pages.text:
Flash goes the lightning bright,
Six... five... four... three... two...
Malik searches left and right
For the seven that he knew.

Under pebbles smooth and round,
Behind the driftwood silver-grey,
In the tide pools, safe and sound,
Where the sea sprites love to play.

Maybe seven's feeling small,
Maybe seven's scared to call.

illustration.description: Malik, a boy with short twists, dark brown skin, and gentle expressive eyes, searches among glowing mushrooms and magical pebbles on the beach. Tiny mystical creatures peek out from tide pools filled with golden sparkles. The storm rages overhead in rich purples while enchanted forest art style details show fairy lights illuminating the detailed foliage and rocks, Studio Ghibli inspired magical atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Malik searching for the missing seven among magical beach elements

pages.text:
Flash goes the lightning bright,
Six... five... four... three... two...
Malik whispers soft and light,
"Seven, I believe in you."

"Come back, seven, take your turn,
The storm song needs you most of all,
Not to win, but just to learn
How to answer thunder's call."

Seven rumbles, shy but true,
Seven's home, the song's brand new!

Flash goes the lightning bright,
Seven... six... five... four... three... two... ONE!

illustration.description: Malik, a boy with short twists, dark brown skin, and gentle expressive eyes, stands with arms outstretched as magical number seven appears in glowing light beside him. The storm celebrates around them with mystical creatures dancing, golden sparkles swirling through the air. The enchanted forest art style shows the complete thunder team reunited with rich greens and purples, fairy lights twinkling, Studio Ghibli inspired magical atmosphere with detailed foliage celebrating.
illustration.alt: Malik welcoming seven back as the thunder counting game is complete again

metadata.illustrationStyle: enchanted forest, glowing mushrooms, fairy lights, mystical creatures, rich greens and purples, golden sparkles, Studio Ghibli inspired, magical atmosphere, detailed foliage

coverPrompt.description: Malik, a boy with short twists, dark brown skin, and gentle expressive eyes, stands on a pebble beach as dark storm clouds gather overhead. Lightning flickers in the distance while magical sparkles dance between the raindrops. The scene has an enchanted forest art style with rich purples and golden sparkles, Studio Ghibli inspired magical atmosphere.
coverPrompt.alt: A young boy on a stormy beach with magical elements swirling around him

questions.question: What number was missing from the thunder's counting song?
questions.explanation: Seven was the missing number that Malik noticed had disappeared from the storm's counting pattern.

questions.question: Where does this story take place?
questions.explanation: The story happens on a pebble beach where Malik listens to the storm and notices the missing number.

questions.question: Who noticed that seven was missing?
questions.explanation: Only Malik noticed that seven was missing from the thunder's counting song, while his parents didn't see the problem.

questions.question: How did Malik help seven come back?
questions.explanation: Malik whispered softly and kindly to seven, encouraging it to come back and take its turn in the counting song.

questions.question: What happened at the end of the story?
choices: Seven returned to complete the song
questions.explanation: Seven came back and the thunder's counting song was complete again, making the storm whole.
